text.title: Trunk-Based Development Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 5-day branch with 1200-line diff; conflicts pile up; merge takes a full day.
antipattern.root-cause: Feature was not chunked; flag infra not used.
antipattern.fix: Apply trunk-based-branch-by-abstraction. Chunk into < 24h PRs behind a flag. Squash on merge.

antipattern.symptom: Flag 'billing.disable_legacy' is renamed to 'billing.use_new' mid-rollout; engineers ship two changes that interpret it opposite ways.
antipattern.root-cause: Boolean-negative naming inverts logic on rename.
antipattern.fix: Start with positive naming (use_, enable_, show_). Never start a new flag with disable_ / no_ / not_.

antipattern.symptom: Audit finds 47 active flags; no one knows what 12 of them gate.
antipattern.root-cause: Cleanup tickets never filed; no inventory.
antipattern.fix: Require .aidocs/feature-flags.md entry per flag with task id + cleanup ticket. Weekly stale-flag review until < 10 stale.

antipattern.symptom: Flag flipped to 100% on Friday; Monday morning the data team finds silent decimal corruption.
antipattern.fix: Always ramp 0% -> < 1% (diurnal cycle) -> 5% -> 25% -> 100%, with metric comparison at each step. Hold or rollback on diverging metrics.

antipattern.symptom: Trunk red at 2pm; revert happens at 5pm because original author is in another timezone.
antipattern.fix: Install auto-revert action: red CI on main = bot revert PR within 15 min; original author re-opens with the real fix.
